summary: Phase IV.1 — reply/quote/edit messages, edit history, /api/ws/user, notification service. Backend pytest 17/17 PASS. Frontend: reply/edit UI verified at code-level (testids present and correctly conditional on own messages). Live badge end-to-end UI assertion deferred due to test-environment auto-mark-read timing (see RCA below); backend WS proves the underlying flow works.

integration_issues.flow: Post-login redirect to last route + UnreadContext init
integration_issues.issue: After Marcus logs in, the app restores the last route (/messages/c/<conv_id>). The Messages page mounts the conversation, which auto-calls POST /api/conversations/<id>/read, which resets unread to 0 before the user ever sees the global badge. This is correct behavior in production (open conv = read), but it makes the badge race against UnreadContext's initial GET. Recommend: skip auto-mark-read for ~500ms after mount, or only mark-read on first user-interaction (scroll/focus/send).

action_items: Consider delaying auto-mark-read on Messages conversation open (small debounce or first-interaction) so the global nav badge has time to display when a user lands directly on a deep-linked conversation route.

Optional: have UnreadContext expose a force-refresh hook tied to route changes so the conv-list panel and the nav badge never disagree (currently the conv list shows unread counts from /api/conversations which is a snapshot, and the badge shows total from /api/conversations/unread/count which can be racing).
critical_code_review_comments: messaging.py post_message (lines 497-517): clean two-channel fan-out — per-conv WS broadcast for live message render, plus per-user WS broadcast_user for the unread badge delta on each non-sender member. Notifications dispatched via services.notifications_service.dispatch, removing direct db.notifications writes. Solid abstraction.

messaging.py edit_message (522-550): correct authorization order (membership → 404 if missing → 403 if not sender → 400 if empty). Pushes the previous content+timestamp into edit_history before overwriting. Broadcasts {type:'message_edited', message:enriched} so other tabs/clients update in place. Good.
messaging.py message_history (553-569): returns history array with original content as history[0]. Non-members blocked via _assert_member. Good.

messaging.py reply validation (lines 454-463): correctly rejects reply_to_id pointing at a message from a different conversation with 400. Snippet truncated to 140 chars and falls back to '📎 Attachment' if parent has no text.

notifications_service.py: clean provider abstraction with InAppProvider (writes to notifications collection + WS push to recipient) and EmailProviderStub (logs only). dispatch() catches per-provider exceptions so one bad provider can't poison the chain. Resend onboarding path documented in module docstring — single-file addition, no call-site changes. Excellent design.

realtime.py: added connect_user / disconnect_user / broadcast_user mapping user_id → set of WS connections. In-process only (same caveat as Phase IV — won't fan out across uvicorn workers in horizontal scaling).

Sidebar.jsx (lines 102, 148-170): NavItem now takes a badge prop; badge renders only when truthy with capped '99+'. data-testid='nav-messages-badge' wired correctly. Visually conditional — disappears when total=0.

Messages.jsx: reply-btn-<id> wired on EVERY message (so anyone can reply to anyone), edit-btn-<id> guarded by isOwn check (line ~445) — only own messages get the edit affordance. reply-preview and edit-preview chips render in composer with correct testids. The message_edited WS handler at line 109 mutates the message list in place, so live edits update without a refetch.

UnreadContext.jsx: opens /api/ws/user using BACKEND_URL with http→ws swap and forwards 'unread' events (delta or reset) into local state. 'notification' events are re-emitted as a 'synaptiq:notification' window CustomEvent for the bell to subscribe to — nice decoupling. One nit: the WS connection isn't reconnect-aware (no exponential backoff on close); a dropped connection means the badge stops updating until the user reloads. Worth adding a simple retry loop in a follow-up.
Auto-mark-read on conversation open races with the initial unread fetch (see integration_issues). Not a functional bug but creates a confusing UX when deep-linking to a conv.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_phase4_1_test.py (new — 17 pytest tests covering regression, reply, edit, edit-history, notifications, /api/ws/user cookie auth + unread delta + reset, message_edited WS broadcast)

success_rate.frontend: Code-level verified; live badge end-to-end blocked by auto-mark-read timing (not a code bug)

test_credentials: elena.varga@synaptiq.io / demo123, marcus.okafor@synaptiq.io / demo123, aiko.tanaka@synaptiq.io / demo123
seed_data_creation: Tests create parent/reply/edit messages in the Elena↔Marcus and Elena↔Aiko direct conversations. All data is namespaced via content prefixes ('PARENT-MSG…', 'v1 original', 'orig content A', etc.) — no teardown required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase IV.1 backend is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/backend_phase4_1_test.py (17 tests, all passing). Run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/backend_phase4_1_test.py -v --asyncio-mode=auto. Requires websockets + pytest-asyncio (already installed). The frontend code review confirms all required testids (nav-messages-badge, reply-btn-<id>, reply-preview, edit-btn-<id>, edit-preview, message-edited-<id>) are wired correctly in Sidebar.jsx and Messages.jsx. The only flaky/timing concern in UI testing is the auto-mark-read on conversation open clearing the badge before a Playwright screenshot can capture it. To reproduce the live badge: log in as Marcus, FIRST navigate to /discover (clear localStorage to avoid prior-route restore), THEN have an external session (curl) post an Elena→Marcus message — the WS push will increment the badge in real-time.
rca of the issue: The backend WS test (test_ws_user_with_cookie_accepts_and_unread_delta) proves the {type:'unread', conversation_id, delta:1} event is broadcast to Marcus's /api/ws/user channel when Elena posts to a conv Marcus is a member of. UnreadContext.jsx subscribes to that and updates total. The frontend race in our Playwright run is environmental: Marcus's prior session left his last-viewed URL as /messages/c/<elena-conv>, so AuthContext-restored navigation lands him directly inside the conv, the Messages page auto-calls POST /api/conversations/<id>/read on mount, the server pushes {type:'unread', reset:true} on his user channel, and total drops to 0 before we can screenshot the badge. This is correct production behavior (open conv = read), not a bug. Mitigation for cleaner UX in deep-link landing: tiny debounce (500ms) before issuing the auto-mark-read so the badge briefly renders, OR mark-read on first user interaction (scroll/focus) instead of on mount.
